channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hiker accidentally uncovers 280-million-year-old footprints in Italy
item.description: Hikers were responsible for the discovery of what scientists believe to be a prehistoric ecosystem. The first sign of the ecosystem discovered by the hikers were footprints.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former British soldier who broke out of prison is convicted of spying for Iran
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A former British soldier whose audacious escape from a London prison spurred a dayslong search was convicted on Thursday of spying for Iran.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian court orders recount of first-round election won by far-right outsider
item.description: A top Romanian court has asked the official electoral authority to recount and verify all of the ballots cast in the first round of the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A social media ban for under-16s passes the Australian Senate and will soon be a world-first law
item.description: The Australian Senate has passed a social media ban for young children that will soon become a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands of Lebanese return to their homes as Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes hold
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — Thousands of Lebanese displaced by the war between Israel and Hezbollah militants returned home Wednesday as a ceasefire took hold, driving cars stacked with personal belongings and defying warnings from Lebanese and Israeli troops to avoid some areas.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adani fraud allegations rock Indian Parliament as opposition demands probe into billionaire's firms
item.description: India’s opposition has disrupted Parliament for a third day, protesting the government’s silence over allegations against billionaire Gautam Adani, who was recently indicted in the U.S. for alleged fraud and a scheme to pay bribes.
item.dc:creator: Ashok Sharma, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Latest 'massive' Russian aerial attack cuts power to 1 million homes in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian officials say Russia has conducted a “massive” attack against Ukraine’s energy infrastructure, firing nearly 200 missiles and drones and leaving more than a million households without power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Philippine police file criminal complaints against Sara Duterte and her security aides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police officials on Wednesday filed criminal complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and her security staff for allegedly assaulting authorities and disobeying orders in a recent altercation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC prosecutor requests arrest warrant for the head of Myanmar's military regime
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor asked judges on Wednesday to issue an arrest warrant for the head of Myanmar’s military regime for crimes committed against the country’s Rohingya Muslim minority. Senior Gen.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: State Department downgrades travel advisory for China
item.description: The State Department on Wednesday downgraded its travel advisory for China, shortly after the White House announced it had secured the release of three Americans as part of a prisoner swap with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Russia to US: Halt 'spiral of escalation' over Ukraine
item.description: Russia on Wednesday warned the U.S. to stop what it said was a “spiral of escalation” over Ukraine as Washington moves to quickly supply Kyiv with more weapons as the Biden administration winds down.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Democrat calls for sanctions over violent clashes in Pakistan
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) denounced Pakistan’s chief of army staff Wednesday for allegedly killing citizens and confiscating death records from medics in an effort to subdue growing protests in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: NATO chief: Ukraine not in strong position to negotiate with Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said on Wednesday that Ukraine is not in a strong enough position to begin negotiating an end to the war with Russia and said any deal struck must “prevent the Russians from getting what they want.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Australian police officer who used Taser on 95-year-old woman found guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Concerns over housing and immigration make for a volatile campaign in Ireland's election
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — From his poster, there’s not much to distinguish Gerry Hutch from the slew of other independent candidates hoping discontented voters will propel them into Ireland’s parliament in an election on Friday. “We need change, and I’m your man,” it says.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that went into effect on Wednesday could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah militant group, raising hopes and renewing difficult questions in a region gripped by conflict. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon begins
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah militants began early Wednesday morning, after Beirut residents endured the most intense day of Israeli strikes since the war began. Many wondered if the agreement to stop fighting would hold.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G7 ministers throw support behind Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire but make no mention of ICC warrant
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from leading industrialized countries threw their strong support Tuesday behind an immediate ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah but sidestepped the question of whether to enforce an international arrest warrant for Israel’s leade
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured in UK after 20 years
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years. San Diego, 46, wanted for his alleged involvement in two bombings in Northern California in the 2000s, was arrested in Wales, the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New subway system in Greece puts archaeological finds discovered during construction on display for passengers
item.description: A new subway system is on its way to being showcased to passengers in Thessaloniki, Greece. The project unveiled thousands of ancient finds through excavations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces cease-fire plan between Israel and Hezbollah ending 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced his support for a cease-fire deal that has yet to be finalized between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 dead as protests erupt in Pakistan over jailed former Prime Minister Imran Khan
item.description: Six people were killed as protests supporting imprisoned former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan escalated in Islamabad on Tuesday, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Congress looking to put suffocating sanctions on 'kangaroo' ICC over Netanyahu arrest warrant
item.description: After the ICC’s legal assault on the Mideast’s only democracy, Israel, for its war on Hamas terrorism, a powerful U.S. legislative and executive campaign has emerged against the controversial body.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Uruguay ousts conservative government, elects leftist opposition candidate as turnout hits 90 percent
item.description: Leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi claims victory in Uruguay's run-off presidential election, defeating center-right candidate Álvaro Delgado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel moves toward ceasefire deal with Hezbollah: reports
item.description: Israel is reportedly moving toward a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon after nearly a year of fighting escalated into an all-out war
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four men convicted in Spain in connection to 2021 hate crime murder
item.description: Four men were sentenced for their involvement in the homophobic murder of 24-year-old Samuel Luiz, who died at a hospital in July 2021 after being attacked for allegedly being gay.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee: Hostages must be released
item.description: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee criticized the U.S. on Friday for pressuring Israel instead of holding Hamas accountable.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9
